page.title: Assumption Day Yacht Charter in Marbella: A Mid-August Maritime Retreat

data.meta_description: Escape the August crowds with an Assumption Day yacht charter in Marbella. Cruise the Costa del Sol from €749/2h. Book your mid-August maritime retreat now.
data.summary: Discover how to celebrate Assumption Day (15 August) on the water with a yacht charter in Marbella. Beat the crowds, enjoy free-flowing drinks, and explore hidden coves from Puerto Banús.
key_facts: Assumption Day (15 August) is a public holiday in Spain, making it ideal for a long weekend charter.
Our fleet departs from Puerto Banús, Marbella’s premier marina.
Prices start at €749 for 2 hours on an Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 (skipper, drinks, VAT included).
Tier B charters (Mangusta 80) from €4,719 for 4 hours, with a free jet ski included.
All charters include licensed skipper, fuel, soft drinks, beer, cava, snacks, and insurance.
Popular mid-August destinations: Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, Cabopino, and Estepona.
August sea temperatures average 24–26°C, perfect for swimming and water sports.
Book early — mid-August is peak season and availability is limited.

faq.q: What is Assumption Day and why charter a yacht in Marbella?
faq.a: Assumption Day (15 August) is a national holiday in Spain, marking the Assumption of the Virgin Mary. It falls in peak summer, so locals and tourists alike seek escapes from crowded beaches. A yacht charter from Puerto Banús offers a private, air-conditioned retreat on the sea, with swimming stops, sunbathing, and drinks included.

faq.q: How much does an Assumption Day yacht charter cost?
faq.a: Our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) start at €749 for 2 hours, including skipper, drinks, snacks, and 21% IVA. A full day (8 hours) is €2,299. For larger groups, Tier B boats like the Mangusta 80 are €4,719 for a minimum 4 hours, with a jet ski thrown in. Other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish VAT (21%). There are no hidden fees — just show up at Puerto Banús and step aboard.

faq.q: Which boats are available for Assumption Day?
faq.a: We have 17 boats departing from Puerto Banús, from the 6.4m Mariah SX21 (5 pax) to the 29m Ferretti 94 (12 pax). Popular mid-August choices include the Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) for its spacious sun deck, and the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) for a luxury experience with a free jet ski.

faq.q: Where can we sail on Assumption Day?
faq.a: Typical itineraries head east to Cabopino or Cala del Faro, or west to Cala Cortés, Estepona, and Sotogrande. Your skipper will recommend the best route based on sea conditions and your preferences. All departures are from Puerto Banús, Marbella.

faq.q: Is August a good time for a yacht charter in Marbella?
faq.a: Absolutely. August offers long sunny days, sea temperatures of 24–26°C, and minimal wind. The only downside is crowds on land, but a yacht charter lets you escape to quiet coves. Assumption Day itself is a public holiday, so book early to secure your preferred boat.

faq.q: How do I book an Assumption Day charter?
faq.a: Contact us via WhatsApp or the booking form on our website. Provide your preferred date, group size, and any special requests. We’ll confirm availability and send you a payment link. A deposit secures the booking, with the balance due 48 hours before departure.

faq.q: Can we bring our own food or decorations for Assumption Day?
faq.a: Yes, you’re welcome to bring your own food and decorations. Our standard catering includes light snacks and drinks, but you can order a catered menu or bring a picnic. Just let us know in advance so we can accommodate storage and serving.

data.body_html:
Assumption Day (15 August) is one of Spain’s most important public holidays — and in Marbella, it falls right in the middle of the sweltering high season. While the beaches of the Golden Mile and Puerto Banús are packed with sun-seekers, the smartest way to celebrate is on the water. An Assumption Day yacht charter in Marbella gives you a private, air-conditioned escape from the crowds, with swimming stops, unlimited drinks, and the freedom to explore the Costa del Sol at your own pace. Our fleet of 17 boats departs from Puerto Banús, and prices start at just €749 for two hours — skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks included. Whether you’re planning a family day out or a glamorous getaway with friends, here’s everything you need to know to make the most of the mid-August holiday.

Why Choose an Assumption Day Yacht Charter in Marbella?

Marbella in August is glorious — blue skies, 30°C heat, and a vibrant social scene. But it’s also crowded. Assumption Day is a national holiday, so every beach bar, chiringuito, and stretch of sand is heaving. A private yacht charter solves that problem instantly. You get your own floating terrace with a cool breeze, a shaded saloon, and the ability to anchor in quiet coves inaccessible to day-trippers. Plus, with our all-inclusive pricing (skipper, drinks, snacks, VAT), there are no surprises. You just show up at Puerto Banús and relax.

Our Fleet for Assumption Day

We operate 17 boats from Puerto Banús, ranging from nimble speedboats to superyachts. All are maintained to the highest standard and come with a licensed skipper. Here are the most popular choices for a mid-August charter:

  • Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) — €749/2h to €2,299/8h. Ideal for small groups and families.
  • Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) — same pricing as Astondoa 40. A sporty, elegant cruiser.
  • Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) — from €4,719 for 4 hours, includes a free jet ski. Perfect for a luxury day out.
  • Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) — spacious flybridge for sunbathing, with a large cockpit for dining.
  • Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) — the flagship of our fleet, with a jacuzzi and full crew.
  • Bandido (11.5m, 8 pax) — a classic open boat for a sporty, wind-in-your-hair experience.

For pricing on any boat not listed above, simply contact us via WhatsApp for a personalised quote.

Best Itineraries for Assumption Day

Your skipper will tailor the route to your wishes, but here are three classic options from Puerto Banús:

  • East to Cabopino & Cala del Faro — a 30-minute cruise to the dunes of Cabopino, then anchor at Cala del Faro for swimming. Total round trip 3–4 hours.
  • West to Cala Cortés & Estepona — head past the Golden Mile and the Río Verde towards Cala Cortés, a rocky cove with crystal-clear water. Continue to Estepona port for lunch. Allow 5–6 hours.
  • South to the open sea — for those who want to feel the Mediterranean swell, a longer cruise towards Sotogrande or even Gibraltar (weather permitting). Full-day charter recommended.

All itineraries include stops for swimming, snorkelling, and sunbathing. Your skipper knows the best spots to avoid the holiday crowds.

What’s Included in Your Charter

Transparency is key — there are no hidden extras. Every charter includes:

  • Licensed, English-speaking skipper
  • Fuel for the entire trip
  • Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava
  • Light snacks (olives, crisps, nuts)
  • Insurance and safety equipment
  • Spanish IVA (21% VAT)

You’re welcome to bring your own food, decorations, or a birthday cake. If you want a full catering menu, let us know at least 48 hours in advance and we’ll arrange it with our partner caterers.

How to Book Your Assumption Day Yacht Charter

Mid-August is peak season, and Assumption Day is one of the busiest days of the year. We recommend booking at least two weeks in advance. Here’s how it works:

  1. Browse our fleet on our main page and choose your preferred boat.
  2. Contact us via WhatsApp with your date, group size, and any special requests.
  3. We’ll confirm availability and send a secure payment link for a deposit (usually 50%).
  4. The balance is due 48 hours before departure. On the day, meet your skipper at Puerto Banús at the agreed time.

If you’re unsure which boat suits your group, our team can recommend based on your numbers and budget. For example, a group of 10 friends might love the Mangusta 80 with its jet ski, while a family with young children might prefer the steady Azimut 58 Flybridge.

Tips for a Perfect Assumption Day on the Water

  • Arrive early — Puerto Banús gets busy on holidays. Aim to board by 10:00 to beat the traffic.
  • Bring sunscreen and a hat — the sun is intense in August, and the sea breeze can fool you.
  • Pack swimwear and a towel — every itinerary includes swimming stops.
  • Consider a full-day charter — with 8 hours, you can cruise to a far-flung cove, have lunch on board, and return as the sun sets over La Concha.
  • Book a jet ski add-on — on Tier B boats, a jet ski is included; on others, we can arrange one for an extra fee.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is Assumption Day and why charter a yacht in Marbella?

Assumption Day (15 August) is a national holiday in Spain, marking the Assumption of the Virgin Mary. It falls in peak summer, so locals and tourists alike seek escapes from crowded beaches. A yacht charter from Puerto Banús offers a private, air-conditioned retreat on the sea, with swimming stops, sunbathing, and drinks included.

How much does an Assumption Day yacht charter cost?

Our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) start at €749 for 2 hours, including skipper, drinks, snacks, and 21% IVA. A full day (8 hours) is €2,299. For larger groups, Tier B boats like the Mangusta 80 are €4,719 for a minimum 4 hours, with a jet ski thrown in. Other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp.

What is included in the charter price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish VAT (21%). There are no hidden fees — just show up at Puerto Banús and step aboard.

Which boats are available for Assumption Day?

We have 17 boats departing from Puerto Banús, from the 6.4m Mariah SX21 (5 pax) to the 29m Ferretti 94 (12 pax). Popular mid-August choices include the Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) for its spacious sun deck, and the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) for a luxury experience with a free jet ski.

Where can we sail on Assumption Day?

Typical itineraries head east to Cabopino or Cala del Faro, or west to Cala Cortés, Estepona, and Sotogrande. Your skipper will recommend the best route based on sea conditions and your preferences. All departures are from Puerto Banús, Marbella.

Is August a good time for a yacht charter in Marbella?

Absolutely. August offers long sunny days, sea temperatures of 24–26°C, and minimal wind. The only downside is crowds on land, but a yacht charter lets you escape to quiet coves. Assumption Day itself is a public holiday, so book early to secure your preferred boat.

How do I book an Assumption Day charter?

Contact us via WhatsApp or the booking form on our website. Provide your preferred date, group size, and any special requests. We’ll confirm availability and send you a payment link. A deposit secures the booking, with the balance due 48 hours before departure.

Can we bring our own food or decorations for Assumption Day?

Yes, you’re welcome to bring your own food and decorations. Our standard catering includes light snacks and drinks, but you can order a catered menu or bring a picnic. Just let us know in advance so we can accommodate storage and serving.
